channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple’s iPhone sales broke a new quarterly record during the holidays, despite AI blunders
item.description: Apple’s iPhone sales soared to a new quarterly record during the holiday season, despite artificial intelligence blunders that prompted the technology trendsetter to get a helping hand from Google.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Resilience is essential as demands on government networks multiply
item.description: Agencies can’t predict the next attack, new mandates or the next cloud outage. But they can control the strength of their network foundation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Under Armour data breach claims trigger alerts for millions of users
item.description: Under Armour allegedly faces massive data breach affecting 72 million customers after hackers posted records online following November ransomware attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Forward Networks launches agentic AI system built on network digital twin
item.description: Network operations teams face a flood of AI-powered tools promising simplified management through natural language interfaces. Many amount to conversational wrappers around existing capabilities, offering convenience but not fundamental changes to how networks are managed and verified.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Founding Fathers with extra bounce: New bobblehead set marks America’s 250th
item.description: "There's a lot of people who are really interested in the nation's history and bobbleheads are a perfect way for that," Phil Sklar said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: This whole AI thing is simpler than you think
item.description: At least three-quarters of the speaking invitations I get these days are about AI. But lately, they’re for different reasons. Companies used to bring me in under the assumption that artificial intelligence was going to change everything.
item.dc:creator: Douglas Rushkoff, Fast Company

item.title: Top 11 network outages and application failures of 2025
item.description: System outages plagued organizations of all sizes in 2025, with major incidents exposing vulnerabilities across network infrastructure and the IT dependencies that define today’s distributed environments, according to the latest Internet outage report from Cisco ThousandEyes.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The best free cloud computing courses in 2026
item.description: The cloud isn’t new, but the way enterprises use it keeps evolving. AI workloads, new platform services, and tighter governance mean teams can’t rely on outdated knowledge. The real issue isn’t ignorance but drift.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New PDF compression filter will save space, need software updates
item.description: Brotli is one of the most widely used but least-known compression formats ever devised, long incorporated into all major browsers and web content delivery networks (CDNs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Thousands of iPhone apps expose data inside Apple App Store
item.description: Researchers found Apple iOS app security flaws that expose user data and payment systems despite positive App Store reviews, according to Cybernews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 11 cool things Copilot can do in Excel
item.description: Microsoft has woven its generative AI technology throughout Microsoft 365, the company’s productivity suite. Its Copilot AI assistant is most often used in M365 apps for text-oriented actions, such as generating email or document drafts in Outlook or Word, or writing summaries of Teams meetings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 2026 network outage report and internet health check
item.description: ThousandEyes, a Cisco company, monitors how ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services are handling any performance challenges and provides Network World with a weekly roundup of events that impact service delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI robot brings emotional care to pets
item.description: Aura AI pet robot tracks behavior changes to detect when pets feel excited, anxious or relaxed. Tuya Smart's device offers laser play, treat dispensing and voice interaction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The drone economy is about to take off fast: A $355B market and new rule could make drones-as-a-service the next big thing
item.description: "It allows drones to be operated beyond the pilot's visual line of sight, and it allows multiple drones to operate by one operator or pilot," McDanolds said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel wrestling with CPU supply shortage
item.description: Intel said that it is facing a CPU shortage, particularly affecting the data center/server business, but that it expects the problem to peak this quarter and ease throughout the year.
item.dc:creator: Network World
